title: Heap Allocation Contracts and Failure Handling · Spiral Systems Review
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the allocation wrapper with explicit outcomes contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for allocation wrapper with explicit outcomes after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply malloc and calloc size, initialization, alignment, zero-size, and failure contracts without dereferencing assumptions.
Propagate allocation failure without partial-state leaks, null dereferences, or impossible-error assumptions.
Choose and test a project policy for zero elements rather than depending on an implementation-specific returned pointer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Sized Free C23
steps.instruction:
review case — A review native-systems team handles memory case 9484. Produce bounded C evidence to handle allocation failure and zero-size policy explicitly; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Adopt C23 free_sized and free_aligned_sized only behind exact size, alignment, availability, and fallback contracts.
